failure_surface: AttributeError during code execution: 'Worksheet' object has no attribute 'max_col'. Agent used incorrect attribute name when accessing the maximum column count from openpyxl worksheet object.
root_cause: Agent incorrectly referenced the openpyxl Worksheet attribute as 'max_col' instead of the correct 'max_column'. This indicates incomplete knowledge of the openpyxl library's Worksheet API, leading to runtime failure on first solution attempt.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before writing code with external libraries, verify the exact attribute and method names through documentation or quick inspection. Use consistent naming conventions specific to each library rather than assuming shortened forms.
evidence: First solution code contained: print(f"New sheet dimensions: {ws.max_row} rows x {ws.max_col} columns")
Error traceback explicitly states: AttributeError: 'Worksheet' object has no attribute 'max_col'
Second attempt corrected the typo to 'max_column' and executed successfully
Initial exploration code also correctly used 'max_column' earlier in the same session
SKILL.md documents openpyxl usage but does not enumerate common Worksheet properties

memory_items.title: Verify External Library APIs Before Coding
memory_items.lesson: When using spreadsheet manipulation libraries (openpyxl, pandas, xlrd), confirm the exact attribute and method names exist for your version before writing production code.
memory_items.generalization: Common Spreadsheet Attribute Pattern: Most Excel-related libraries use descriptive property names (e.g., max_row, max_column). Do not assume abbreviated forms unless documented.

memory_items.title: Test API Properties During Exploration Phase
memory_items.lesson: During initial spreadsheet inspection, exercise all commonly-used properties (max_row, max_column, cell value retrieval) to establish working patterns before implementing the full solution.
memory_items.generalization: Exploration Hygiene: When examining a workbook's structure, test the complete set of dimension/access methods you plan to use later to avoid surprise errors during final implementation.

memory_items.title: Handle Attribute Name Variations Across Libraries
memory_items.lesson: Different spreadsheet libraries may use different naming conventions (e.g., pandas DataFrame vs openpyxl Worksheet). Document or store successful patterns once verified.
memory_items.generalization: Library-Specific Naming Awareness: Attribute names are library-dependent. Maintain mental or written reference for common operations per library to prevent cross-library contamination.

edits.content: - **API Property Names**: When accessing Worksheet properties like row/column counts, use exact openpyxl attribute names: 'max_row' and 'max_column' (not abbreviated forms like 'max_col'). Verify available attributes via dir() or documentation before coding complex solutions.

- **Dimension Verification**: Always test dimension-accessing methods (max_row, max_column) during initial exploration phase to confirm they work with the loaded workbook version before using them in loop ranges or conditionals.
